mission.title: podman-container-tools: [RFE]Make docker-compose work with rootless podman
mission.description: [RFE]Make docker-compose work with rootless podman. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current podman-container-tools setup
steps.description:
Verify your podman-container-tools version and configuration:
```bash
podman --version
```
This feature requires a working podman-container-tools installation.

steps.title: Review podman-container-tools configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant podman-container-tools configuration:
I've tried docker-compose with rootless podman, but that doesn't currently work:

```
siretart:~/git/awesome-compose/gitea-postgres$ docker-compose -H unix:/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock up
Pulling gitea (gitea/gitea:latest)...

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFE]Make docker-compose work with rootless podman
steps.description:
Instead of creating an extra container create a network and mount
namespace inside the podman user namespace. This ns is used to
for rootless cni operations.
This helps to align the rootless and rootful network code path.
If we run as rootless we just have to set up a extra net ns and
initialize

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "[RFE]Make docker-compose work with rootless podman" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Instead of creating an extra container create a network and mount
namespace inside the podman user namespace. This ns is used to
for rootless cni operations.
This helps to align the rootless and rootful network code path.
If we run as rootless we just have to set up a extra net ns and
initialize slirp4netns in it. The ocicni lib will be called in
that net ns.
